data.title: MGM China Logs Higher Profit, Revenue in 2024; Shares Slide 5%
data.description:
MGM China's attributable profit rose to HK$4.60 billion in 2024 from HK$2.64 billion in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the casino operator increased to HK$1.206 from HK$0.692 a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS at HK$1.24.

Operating revenue grew to HK$31.4 billion from HK$24.7 billion in the previous year. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at HK$30.8 billion.

The company declared a final dividend of HK$0.251 per share, payable June 12 to shareholders on record as of June 3.

Shares slid 5% during Friday's late morning trading.

data.title: China National Chemical Engineering Secures 53 Billion Yuan Contracts in January-February Period
data.description:
China National Chemical Engineering reported a total contract value of 53.4 billion yuan in the first two months of 2025.

Domestic contracts totaled 40.5 billion yuan, while overseas contracts reached 12.9 billion yuan, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the chemical company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Saudi Regulator OKs Dar Al Majed Real Estate's IPO on Tadawul
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's Capital Market Authority approved Dar Al Majed Real Estate Co.'s registration and initial public offering on Tadawul.

The real estate company plans to offer 90 million shares, corresponding to 30% of its share capital, according to a Thursday release.

The regulator's approval is valid for six months and does not imply any recommendation to invest in the company's shares.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea's Total Debt Hits Record KRW6,222 Trillion
data.description:
South Korea's combined debt, encompassing government, corporate, and household borrowing, has reached a record 6,222 trillion won at the end of the third quarter of 2024, up 4.1% from a year earlier, Yonhap News Agency reported Thursday, citing Bank for International Settlements data.

Corporate debt increased 2.9% to 2,798 trillion won, household borrowing rose 2.1% to hit 2,283 trillion won, while government debt surged 11.8% to 1,141 trillion won in the reported quarter from a year earlier, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: South Korean Won Slips for 4th Session
data.description:
The South Korean won continued its downward trend toward 1,470 per dollar, extending losses for the fourth straight session, pressured by rising domestic economic concerns and global trade uncertainties.

Markets braced for the April 2 deadline for Trump's reciprocal tariffs on key trading partners, intensifying trade war concerns.

A stronger dollar also added pressure after the Federal Reserve signaled no rush to cut interest rates further.

Domestically, escalating political turmoil weighed on sentiment following the Democratic Party’s move to impeach acting President Choi Sang-Mok.

Additionally, the Bank of Korea’s latest meeting minutes highlighted concerns about a potential economic slowdown and recently cautioned over market volatility due to US policy uncertainty.

Meanwhile, the Acting President emphasized 24-hour monitoring to protect South Korea’s credit rating amid global shifts and potential US tariffs.

data.title: Marvel Gold to Acquire Tanzanian Resources Company; Shares Down 9%
data.description:
Marvel Gold signed two binding agreements to acquire 100% of the issued capital of Tanzanian company Cobra Resources for $200,000 cash and the issue of company shares worth $175,000 upon completion, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Cobra Resources owns the Hanang gold project, where multiple high-priority targets were identified, the filing said.

The company also received firm commitments to raise AU$4.2 million through a two-tranche placement of 529.4 million shares priced at AU$0.008 apiece.

The proceeds will be used to fund the acquisition, exploration activities at the Hanang project, and general working capital, per the filing.

Marvel Gold shares were down 9% in afternoon trade Friday.

data.title: Malaysia Food Inflation Stays at 3-Month Low
data.description:
Food prices in Malaysia increased by 2.5% year-on-year in February 2025, holding steady for the second consecutive month while remaining at their lowest level in three months.

Cost continued to rise for food at home (0.5% vs 0.4% in January), cereals & cereal products (0.2% vs 0.3%), fish & other seafood (1.2% vs 1.0%), oils & fats (1.7% vs 1.5%), fruits & nuts (0.3% vs 0.3%), sugar, confectionery & desserts (1.1% vs 0.8%), and food away from home (4.6% vs 5.0%).

At the same time, prices declined for meat (-0.3% vs -1.0%), milk, other dairy products & eggs (-0.8% vs -0.4%), and vegetables (-1.0% vs -1.1%).

data.title: Asian stocks slide as growth, tariff worries weigh
data.description:
Key points:
  • Asian stocks drop, European futures point to subdued open
  • Gold huddled near record high on safe haven flows
  • Fed's cautious tone provides dollar support
  • Investor focus turns to details of Trump's reciprocal tariffs

Asian stocks fell on Friday in a downbeat end to the week as deepening geopolitical worries and fears over U.S. tariffs and their impact on the global economy curbed investors appetite for risk, keeping safe-haven gold near record highs.

Futures for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq were steady in Asian hours, while European futures pointed to a weak open.

Policymakers across the globe struck a cautious note in a week filled with central bank meetings as uncertainty in global economics and politics grew. The U.S. Federal Reserve, the Bank Of Japan and the Bank of England all held rates steady.

Central bankers highlighted the unsettled outlook due largely to rising trade tensions triggered by the U.S under President Donald Trump. Trump intends to impose new reciprocal tariff rates on April 2, ushering in fresh wave of uncertainty.

Reports of Israeli airstrikes on Gaza and a huge blast from a Ukrainian drone attack on a Russian military airfield were a reminder of rising geopolitical tensions pushing investors towards safe haven assets.

"With the bar for near-term rate cuts still high, markets have shifted focus back to growth concerns and tariff risks which will continue to fuel volatility," said Charu Chanana, Saxo's chief investment strategist.

MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fell 0.85%, with stocks in China, Hong Kong, Taiwan and Indonesia dropping sharply.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng index (.HIS) slid over 2%, poised for its second straight week in the red as investors turned cautious following a surge in tech stocks and the index hitting a three-year high on Tuesday. The Hang Seng is still up 18% for the year, the best performing major stock market in the world.

Japan's Nikkei , however, was up 0.3% and the broader Topix index touched an eight-month high led by banking stocks as stronger-than-expected inflation data fuelled expectations for further interest rate hikes by the Bank of Japan.

Investors will now focus on the details of the Trump administration's April 2 tariffs as markets become increasingly nervous about the impact of tit-for-tat tariffs on inflation and economic growth.

Ray Sharma-Ong, head of multi-asset investment solutions for Southeast Asia at Aberdeen Investments, said the main uncertainty revolves around the size of reciprocal tariffs, which may lead to markets repricing further downside risks to growth.

The growing uncertainty and the Fed reiterating that it was in no rush to cut rates lent support to the dollar. The dollar index measure against a basket of six counterparts was steady at 103.84, after climbing 0.36% on Thursday.

The index fell to a five-month low this week as earlier hopes for growth-friendly policies under Trump have given way to anxiety that the global trade war he started could trigger a U.S. recession.

The yen was slightly weaker on the day at 149.20 per dollar but stayed close to the near five-month high of 146.545 touched last week. The yen is up 5% this year on expectations that the BOJ will hike rates again in 2025.

Data showed Japan's core inflation hit 3.0% in February and an index stripping away the effect of fuel rose at the fastest pace in nearly a year, a sign of broadening price pressure that reinforces market expectations of further hikes.

"Although Governor Ueda made much of the risks surrounding U.S. trade policy on Wednesday, we think he’s just hedging his bets - considering it a risk factor," said Min Joo Kang, senior economist at ING.

"Therefore, if trade tensions don't escalate more than the market currently expects, they won't affect the BOJ's rate hike plans."

In commodities, oil prices rose on Friday, poised for their strongest weekly performance since January.

Brent crude futures climbed 0.5%, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures were up 0.6%. Both were set for 2% gains for the week.

Gold eased 0.4% to $3,031.5 in Asian hours, just below the record high of the previous session but on course for the third straight week of gains, supported by safe-haven demand.

data.title: JuicyChat.AI Launches NSFW and Hentai AI Chatbots in 2025, Redefining Virtual Roleplay
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 21, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — JuicyChat.AI unveiled its NSFW and Hentai AI Chatbots, combining advanced natural language processing (NLP) with hyper-personalized roleplay scenarios to meet surging demand for immersive virtual interactions. The platform targets users seeking anime-inspired, gaming, and fantasy-driven companionship, aligning with a $30 billion global AI companionship market projected by McKinsey & Company.

AI Technology Powers Dynamic Character Interactions

Trained on 15 million hours of dialogue data, the chatbots use real-time emotion detection and contextual memory to deliver fluid responses. Key features include:

Customizable Personas: Over 200 personality traits (e.g., "tsundere," "yandere") and backstories for anime, manga, and original fantasy characters.

Scenario Builder: Users create branching narratives, with NSFW AI retaining plot details across sessions.

Multimedia Support: AI-generated illustrations accompany text conversations, focusing on artistic, non-explicit visuals.

Freemium Model and User Engagement

The platform offers a free tier (50 daily messages) and premium plans starting at $12.99/month, unlocking:

Uncensored Dialogue: Tailored to mature audiences without explicit content.

Voice Synthesis: Four distinct voice synthesis systems provide users with a seamless blend of visual and auditory enjoyment during conversations.

Exclusive Characters: JuicyChat.AI boasts a vast creator ecosystem, with high-quality NSFW characters added daily.

Privacy-Centric Design and Content Compliance

To address user concerns, JuicyChat.AI implements:

Zero-Data Storage: Conversations deleted after sessions.

Blockchain Age Verification: Mandatory for users under 18.

Triple-Layer Filtering: AI flags inappropriate requests before human review.

Beyond Niche: Broader Use Cases Emerge

While positioned for entertainment, the chatbots show potential in mental health and language learning. A University of Tokyo study found 34% of users reported reduced stress during interactions, while language learners used the dialogue mode to practice slang and honorifics.

Future Innovations

JuicyChat.AI continues to invest in research and development to further enhance its NSFW AI and Hentai AI Chatbot. The company is dedicated to refining algorithms and expanding conversational databases, ensuring richer and more adaptive interactions. With plans for regular updates and feature enhancements, JuicyChat.AI aims to remain at the forefront of conversational technology, offering users an increasingly engaging and personalized chat experience that evolves with emerging trends and user preferences while consistently meeting high performance standards.

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/f0ea98a2-8681-49cf-bba4-6819b7b0fccd

Contact:

JuicyChat.AI

supportteam@juicychat.club

www.juicychat.ai

data.title: Hily Posts Higher FY24 Attributable Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Hily (ADX:HH) said Friday its attributable profit and revenue for full-year 2024 surged year over year.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the company for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 25.2 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 6.5 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.21 dirham from 0.05 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed investment holding company's revenue from contracts with customers was 65.5 million dirhams, compared with 46.3 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Power Outage Forces Closure of London's Heathrow Airport
data.description:
London's Heathrow Airport announced a significant power outage due to a fire at an electrical substation supplying the airport.

The airport will remain closed on Friday for safety reasons, according to a notice posted on its website.

data.title: Manappuram Finance shares jump 2% as Bain Capital set to buy 18% stake for Rs 4,385 crore
data.description:
Manappuram Finance shares jumped nearly 2 percent on March 21 after the company informed exchanges that Bain Capital will be investing Rs 4,385 crore to acquire 18 percent stake in the company. The shares of the company were trading at Rs 221.15 apiece in morning trade.

Manappuram Finance's Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) is set to take place on April 16, where the company will seek shareholders' approval on the preferential allotment and related amendments associated with Articles of Association.

The deal will be executed through a preferential allotment of equity shares (9.29 crore equity shares at Rs 236 per share) and warrants, after completion of which, Bain Capital will be classified as a joint promoter of the company, alongside existing promoters V.P. Nandakumar and Sushama Nandakumar.

Bain Capital will acquire the said stake through its investment arm, BC Asia Investments XXV Limited. Meanwhile an equal number of warrants will be issued to BC Asia Investments XIV Limited at the same price. Each warrant will be convertible into one equity share, with the conversion option available in multiple tranches over four to 18 months.

The deal is subject to shareholder approval and regulatory clearances from the RBI and CCI. If approved, Bain Capital will also gain the right to appoint a director to Manappuram Finance’s board, giving it joint control of the NBFC.

This transaction will lead to a mandatory open offer for a 26 percent stake, increasing Bain Capital's potential holding to over 40 percent. Post the transaction, existing promoters will retain a 28.9 percent stake in the company.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

Nestlé to Cease Operations at Conow Site in Germany Amid Consumer Price Sensitivity, Rising Costs
data.description:
Nestlé said Thursday it plans to cease operations at its production site in Conow, Germany, at the start of 2026 due to consumer price sensitivity and rising costs.

The Swiss consumer goods giant added it is in talks to sell the site to a German company, which expressed interest in taking over both the site and its 80 employees.

Amid the plans for the site, Nestlé said it will relocate the production of Garden Gourmet Vuna, Maggi Liquid Bouillon, and Maggi Texicana Salsa to other locations in Europe.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Friday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Accenture Begins to Feel the Trump Crackdown on Consultants

GSA has asked federal agencies to justify which consulting contracts are mission critical.

Munich Re Taps Into U.S. Market With $2.6 Billion NEXT Insurance Acquisition

The German reinsurer on Thursday said the acquired business will become part of ERGO, the main insurance business of Munich Re.

Billions Flowed Into New Leveraged ETFs Last Year. Now They're in Free Fall.

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Allianz-Led Consortium Buys Viridium for $3.8 Billion

U.S. Treasury Sanctions Network Supporting Iranian Oil Exports

The sanctions are part of the administration's "campaign of maximum pressure on Iran," the department said.

Oil tallies back-to-back gains as Middle East flare-ups lift risk of supply disruption

Crude futures notched back-to-back gains on Thursday, with prices finding support from growing tensions in the Middle East that raise the risk of supply disruptions in the oil-rich region.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0331 GMT - Iron ore falls in early Asian trade. Short-term fundamentals and market expectations continue to show divergent trends, as the outlook for demand remains unclear, analysts at Nanhua Futures write in a note. Weak sentiment is likely to continue adding pressure to prices, they say. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.1% lower at CNY759.0 a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0241 GMT - Palm oil prices fall in Asian trading amid profit-taking ahead of the weekend, Kenanga Futures says in a note. Optimism about recovering exports amid stronger demand by India and China, and Indonesia's higher levy on palm oil could further boost Malaysia's competitiveness in the tropical oil market, it adds. Kenanga Futures sees support for CPO prices at 4,350 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,515 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for June delivery falls 34 ringgit to 4,379 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0154 GMT - Copper rises in early Asian trade. The base metal briefly crossed the psychological level of $10,000 a ton recently, as traders assessed the impact of potential tariffs. Prices could remain supported in the near term due to tightening in the ex-U.S. physical market as Washington's investigation into copper imports continues, ING strategists write in a note. The benchmark three-month LME contract is up 0.5% at $9,984.00 a ton. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0112 GMT - There's likely to be a lid on iron-ore prices without a meaningful improvement in China's domestic steel demand, Citi reckons. The bank sets its 0-3-month forecast at $100/metric ton, highlighting limited supply disruptions in Australia and Brazil, and tipping the pace of supply growth to accelerate. A recent reduction in port stocks and pickup in crude steel output have, among other factors, kept prices from falling below that mark, it says. "Potential supply-side reform of the China steel sector has been top-of-mind for investors with consensus expectations of circa 50 million tons of output cuts this year," Citi says. All else being equal, a cut of that size would be bearish for iron ore, it says. Spot iron ore rose by 0.1% Thursday to $101.75/ton, according to S&P Global Commodity Insights. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0039 GMT - LME three-month copper couldn't hold above $10,000/metric ton, "indicating uncertainty about sustaining prices above this critical threshold," says Sucden Financial's Daria Efanova. The metal briefly broke through that level, hitting a five-month high of $10,046.50/ton, before retreating. A stronger dollar is adding downward pressure, Efanova says. "We believe that the markets will need a stronger fundamental push, in the form of improved demand conditions, to push prices significantly past the $10,120/ton level," she says. The LME three-month copper last fetched $9,910/ton. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0026 GMT - Gold prices are flat in early Asian trade. The precious metal is taking a breather after hitting a record high following Fed Chairman Powell's speech. Despite flagging a high degree of uncertainty around President Trump's policy changes, Powell's speech didn't change market expectations about the path of monetary policy and the likelihood remains that there will be at least two to three rate cuts this year, says Samer Hasn, senior market analyst at XS.com in a note. Gold typically has an inverse relationship with interest rates, as high rates damp demand for the non-interest-bearing metal. Spot gold is flat at $3,045.43/oz. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

1940 GMT - Live cattle futures on the CME close up 1.1% to $2.053 a pound. The June futures contract is now roughly 2 cents a pound below the record high of $2.07 a pound reached in late January. This uptick came despite beef export sales reported by the USDA this morning, which were down 29% from last week's forecast and down 40% from the prior 4-week average. Uncertainty surrounding new tariffs is putting pressure on cattle, analysts say. Lean hogs close up 0.1% 86.1 cents a pound. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1917 GMT - Oil futures post back-to-back gains as the U.S. Treasury steps up enforcement of sanctions on Iranian crude, designating a Chinese refining company that buys Iranian oil and numerous vessels responsible for shipping it. The news lifted prices that were languishing in early trade on a raft of bearish concerns, among them impending U.S. tariffs. "The market could be significantly under-pricing the impact of the upcoming April 2 U.S. reciprocal tariffs on growth and commodity prices," analysts at Citi Research say in a note. "We believe President Trump wants, needs, and will persist in his drive for lower oil prices (including potential domestic oil subsidies/tax cuts) to mitigate the impact of the tariffs on inflation and help bring down interest rates." WTI for April delivery goes off the board at $68.26 a barrel, up 1.6%, and Brent rises 1.7% to $72.00 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1857 GMT - Natural gas futures pull back as the EIA reports a 9 Bcf increase in inventories for last week, reducing the storage deficit against the five-year average to 190 Bcf from 230 Bcf the week before. "While not unprecedented, net injections are rare at this point in the season," Andy Huenefeld of Pinebrook Energy Advisors says in a note, attributing the build to mild weather across key population centers. Futures hit daily lows following the report but "the market has yet to violate the lower boundary of the recent trading range, as prices remain well supported," he adds. The Nymex front month settles down 6.4% at $3.975/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1839 GMT - Front-month gold finishes up 0.1% to $3,040 a troy ounce, a new record--marking the eighth straight session that front-month gold futures settled higher, climbing 5% over that span. Traders remain risk adverse, says Alex Ebkarian of Allegiance Gold. "We continue to see investors gravitating toward safe-haven gold, moving away from riskier investments such as stocks and cryptocurrencies," says Ebkarian. Numerous other economic factors are fueling uncertainty that's making investors uneasy, he adds. SPDR Gold shares are down 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1814 GMT - US-Canadian trade tensions could be a good opportunity for Canada and Canadian retailers to develop their production at home, Elisa Swern, Canadian Consumer Markets Lead at PwC, tells the WSJ. "Canada has the ability to do a lot more than they have been over the last number of years in terms of growing product and servicing different markets," she says. She uses the example of fresh produce, saying that we grow tomatoes, cucumbers, peppers all year round, whether outdoors or in greenhouses. While the capacity would need to catch up, she says that Canada could supply its grocers with what is currently coming from the US. "We might need to build up to it, but we certainly have the capability," says Swern. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1710 GMT - Base metal prices fall, with LME three-month copper down 0.6% at $9,940 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 0.5% at $2,658.50 a ton. Copper briefly exceeded $10,000 a ton earlier in the session, and LME and U.S. commodity exchange Comex prices are up 13% and 26%, respectively, in the year to date. Fears of potential U.S. copper tariffs are creating a bid for the metal, incentivizing flows into the U.S., tightening markets in the rest of the world and dragging LME copper higher, Morgan Stanley analysts say. Fundamentals remain supportive for now, with inventories falling, timespreads tightening and mine supply constrained. However, markets should keep an eye on positioning and be cautious that any backtracking on tariffs could drive an unwind, MS writes. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: China State Construction Declares Final Dividend of HK$0.285 per Share >0830.HK
Novartis Wins US Approval for Oral Fabhalta Treatment for Kidney Disease C3G
data.description:
Novartis won approval from the US Food and Drug Administration for its oral Fabhalta treatment for adults with C3 glomerulopathy, or C3G.

The Swiss pharmaceutical company said Thursday the treatment is the first to be approved for the progressive and ultra-rare kidney disease. The therapy reduces proteinuria, which is thought to be the underlying cause of C3G.

Fabhalta's approval was based on the phase 3 Appear-C3G study that showed clinically meaningful proteinuria reduction.

data.title: New Zealand's Upbeat Q4 2024 GDP Growth Won't Add Upward Pressure to Inflation nor Impact RBNZ's Near-Term Policy Path, Says ANZ Research
data.description:
New Zealand's faster-than-expected GDP growth in the fourth quarter of 2024 leaves room for further expansion in the near term without adding upward pressure to inflation, ANZ Research said in a Thursday note.

GDP grew 0.7% quarter on quarter in the three months to December 2024, ahead of ANZ Research's 0.4% forecast and the Reserve Bank's expectation for a 0.3% print. The services and primary industries topped growth estimates, although ANZ Research noted that both data sets are prone to volatility.

It said that given that growth is coming off a very weak base following a 2.2% contraction in the prior two quarters, the fourth-quarter print is "still consistent with domestic disinflation remaining on a path back to levels consistent with headline inflation at the 2% target midpoint."

The firm does not expect the current data to materially impact the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's policy path and foresees three more rate cuts to bring the official cash rate to 3% by July.

data.title: Hengtong Optic-Electric, Subsidiaries Bag 1.13 Billion Yuan Worth of Projects; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Hengtong Optic-Electric and its subsidiaries won five domestic and overseas projects worth 1.13 billion yuan, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The projects included the Middle East Submarine Cable Project, South China Grid's procurement bid for submarine cables for the Pengbo oilfield shore power, the Guangxi Weizhou Island Undersea interconnection project, the Yuhuan No. 2 and Jinshan wind power projects, and the Weizhou 11-6 oilfield development project in Malaysia, the Chinese fiber optic cable manufacturer said.

The company's shares jumped 4% in recent trade.

data.title: India's Bajaj Finance extends record high rally on 'smooth' top management succession
data.description:
** Shares of India's Bajaj Finance rise as much as 4.7% to record high of 9,089 rupees

** Stock top gainer in benchmark Nifty 50 index , which is down 0.1%

** Non-banking financial co on Thursday named Anup Kumar Saha, present deputy MD as new MD, replacing veteral Rajeev Jain

** Jain named vice chairman, executive director

** Move should allow Jain to stay involved in short- and mid-range planning at Bajaj Finance and investor engagements- Jefferies

** Smooth succession, healthy growth & improving asset quality will aid valuations; BJFN remains top pick - brokerage

** BJFN top gainer in Nifty 50 index so far this year among 22 stocks trading in green, up 31.2% YTD

** In contrast Nifty 50 index down 2.1%

data.title: Malaysia Inflation Rate Hits 13-Month Low
data.description: The annual inflation rate in Malaysia dropped to 1.5% in February 2025 from 1.7% in the previous two months, marking the lowest reading since January 2024 and aligning with market estimates.

data.title: Xref Raises AU$3.6 Million Via Placement; Shares Rise 8%
data.description:
Xref received a commitment from EPICapital (Tanda) to raise AU$3.6 million through the placement of 27.9 million fully paid ordinary shares at AU$0.13 per share, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The placement price represents a 4% premium to the last closing price before Xref's trading halt on March 20 and settlement is expected on March 24, the filing said.

The company will offer a Share Purchase Plan (SPP), aiming to raise up to an additional AU$1 million at an issue price of AU$0.13 per ordinary share, a 4% premium to the last traded price of AU$0.125, the filing added.

Proceeds from the placement and SPP will reduce Xref's net debt and provide flexibility to restructure operations, improve efficiency, and enhance operational cash flow, the company said.

Shares of the company rose 8% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Kuaishou's Fourth-Quarter Net Profit Likely Rose 10% — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Kuaishou Technology is scheduled to report fourth-quarter results on Tuesday. Here's what you need to know:

NET PROFIT FORECAST: The Beijing-based short-video app operator's fourth-quarter net profit likely rose 10.0% to 3.97 billion yuan, equivalent to $547.7 million, according to a poll of seven analysts by FactSet. Its adjusted net profit likely gained 6.9% to 4.66 billion yuan.

REVENUE FORECAST: Kuaishou's revenue likely gained 9.7% to 35.73 billion yuan, supported by its advertising business's strength. That should take Kuaishou's 2024 revenue to 127.24 billion yuan, up 12%.

Kuaishou's shares have risen almost 45% this year so far, buoyed by the positive sentiment over China's AI developments. Shares surged 16% in one session in early March after Chinese officials mentioned its text-to-video AI tool, Kling, during their press conference. Its stocks fell 22% in 2024 on persisting concerns about its e-commerce business amid weak consumer sentiment in China.

WHAT TO WATCH:

  • Kuaishou may need a new growth engine as its e-commerce business slows, Nomura analysts said in a recent note. China's livestreaming e-commerce industry is likely saturated after years of significant growth, as stagnating traffic growth at China's top two short-video apps, Douyin and Kuaishou, could limit their ability to grow audiences, the analysts say. Investors will look for management's comments on its livestreaming e-commerce business and its business priorities for the next year.
  • Kuaishou's text-to-video tool Kling ranked high globally among video generation models, with decent enterprise momentum. Citi analysts think there is attractive potential in this segment due to widespread online video consumption, rising advertisers' demand as well as long-term demand potential from drama and gaming producers.
  • Investors should look for management's 2025 gross merchandise value guidance, a measure of total goods sold. Citi projects 2025 GMV growth at low teens with stable take rates on ads and commission revenue but sees some uncertainty brought by persistent macro softness and competition.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Hanwha Aerospace Shares Tumble After Share-Sale Announcement
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui and Jihye Lee

Hanwha Aerospace's shares tumbled Friday after the company unveiled a plan to raise 3.6 trillion won, equivalent to $2.45 billion, via a share sale.

Shares of the weapons maker plunged as much as 16% to 608,000 won, the lowest intraday level since Feb. 28, according to FactSet. Shares were last quoted 13% down at 631,000 won.

The South Korean defense company plans to issue 5.95 million common shares at 605,000 won per share, it said in a statement after market close Thursday. That's around 16% below Thursday's market closing price.

The company said it expects proceeds from the offering to be approximately 3.6 trillion won. It plans to use the proceeds to invest in its own facilities and acquire stakes in other companies to secure overseas production capabilities, including investing in foreign defense joint ventures and shipbuilders.

Hanwha Aerospace plans to allocate 600 billion won of the proceeds in building a joint venture with a partner in Europe and the Middle East to expand in the region.

This is the largest ever rights offering capital raise in Korea, said Douglas Kim, analyst at Douglas Research Advisory, who publishes on Smartkarma, in commentary.

"This capital raise is likely to result in higher concerns about shares dilution at Hanwha Aerospace," Kim added.

Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com and Jihye Lee at jihye.lee@wsj.com

data.title: India's Bajaj Finance extends rally to record high on 'smooth' top management succession
data.description:
Shares of India's Bajaj Finance rose 3.5% on Friday to extend their record-high rally, a day after it elevated veteran leader and managing director Rajeev Jain to the post of vice chairman, which analysts termed a "smooth succession."

The non-banking financial company also announced present deputy MD Anup Kumar Saha will be replacing Rajeev Jain as MD.

"The move should allow Jain to stay involved in short- and mid-range planning at Bajaj Finance and investor engagements," Jefferies said in a note.

Smooth succession, healthy growth and improving asset quality will aid valuations, the brokerage said, adding that Bajaj Finance remains a top pick.

Bajaj Finance has risen 31% so far this year, and is the top gainer on the Nifty 50 index among 22 stocks in the green. Nifty 50 is down 1.9%.

The stock has been hitting record highs since January after posting strong third-quarter numbers and on expectations of continuing growth momentum.

Analysts, on average, rate Bajaj Finance at "buy," with the median price target at 8,896 rupees.
